# Open Cabinet

> Open Cabinet tracks financial disclosures and stock transactions of U.S. executive branch officials — cabinet secretaries, agency heads, and senior appointees. Data is sourced from the U.S. Office of Government Ethics (OGE) and structured for accountability research.

Built and maintained by Trevor Brown, a journalist and developer (https://trevorthewebdeveloper.com). The project surfaces officials' stock trades, asset holdings, and late filings in a public-interest, conflict-of-interest context.

## What's in the data

- Federal executive branch officials covered by OGE Form 278e disclosures
- Itemized stock transactions: ticker, date, amount range, asset class
- Reported asset holdings and income
- Late-filing flags where periodic transaction reports (PTRs) miss the 45-day deadline

## Primary pages

- /: Overview and dashboard
- /officials: Index of tracked officials
- /officials/[slug]: Per-official detail — transactions, holdings, late filings
- /companies: Tickers held across officials
- /companies/[ticker]: Per-company exposure across officials
- /dashboard: Aggregate trade flow and disclosure activity
- /late-filings: Officials with overdue PTR filings
- /methodology: How the data is collected, normalized, and updated
- /download: Bulk data export

## Sources

- U.S. Office of Government Ethics (OGE) — Form 278e financial disclosures
- Periodic Transaction Reports (PTRs) filed under the STOCK Act
- Public agency rosters for official appointment dates

## Citation

When citing Open Cabinet, please link to https://open-cabinet.org and credit the underlying OGE filings.
